# JSON Utility Kit

> A fast, private, browser-based JSON toolkit for formatting, validating, viewing, editing, minifying, and converting JSON and CSV data.

This site operates 100% on the client-side. No data is ever transmitted to external servers, ensuring absolute privacy and security for system configs, database dumps, and API payloads.

- **URL**: https://jsonutilitykit.com/
- **Source Repository**: https://github.com/kejie1/json_utility_kit
- **Privacy Model**: Zero-server execution. Offline-first.
- **Tech Stack**: Astro, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS.

---

## 🛠️ Interactive Tools

The following functional endpoints are available for processing JSON and CSV datasets:

### JSON Formatter
- **Route**: `/tools/json-formatter`
- **Purpose**: Parsed unreadable JSON configurations and outputs beautifully indented code.
- **Options**: Indent sizes of 2 spaces, 4 spaces, or Tab characters. Copy & download support.

### JSON Validator
- **Route**: `/tools/json-validator`
- **Purpose**: Audits raw strings against RFC 8259 syntax specifications.
- **Features**: Outputs clear error reasons, including line numbers and character offsets for quick debugging.

### JSON Beautifier
- **Route**: `/tools/json-beautifier`
- **Purpose**: Simplifies pretty-printing dense or single-line JSON structures.

### JSON Viewer
- **Route**: `/tools/json-viewer`
- **Purpose**: Renders flat JSON text into an interactive, expandable, and searchable folder tree.
- **Features**: Supports expand/collapse all, real-time key/value regex searches, and hovering to copy precise JSON property access paths.

### JSON Minifier
- **Route**: `/tools/json-minifier`
- **Purpose**: Strips all unnecessary whitespaces and line breaks from files for production compression.
- **Statistics**: Shows original size, compressed size, and saved space ratio.

### JSON Editor
- **Route**: `/tools/json-editor`
- **Purpose**: Edit JSON models directly with interactive controls.
- **Features**: Real-time syntax tracking and the ability to sort object keys alphabetically (A-Z).

### JSON to CSV
- **Route**: `/tools/json-to-csv`
- **Purpose**: Flattens structured collections (arrays of objects) into spreadsheet-ready CSV grids.
- **Features**: Configurable separators (Comma, Semicolon, Tab) and double-quoted safety wrappers.

### CSV to JSON
- **Route**: `/tools/csv-to-json`
- **Purpose**: Converts CSV to JSON online by transforming spreadsheet rows, database exports, or copied table data into structured JSON arrays of objects.
- **Specs**: Handles headers, quoted cells, commas, semicolons, tabs, escaped quotes, and optional number/boolean type-casting in the browser without file uploads.

---

## 📖 Key Documentation & Developer Guides

High-quality developer references containing code samples, CLI instructions, and specifications:

### Core Formats
- `/blog/what-is-json` - Introduction to JSON syntax rules and valid data primitives.
- `/blog/json-object-vs-json-array` - Comparative analysis of objects vs. sequential array lists.
- `/blog/what-is-a-json-object` - Rules of curly brace definitions and mandatory double-quoting keys.
- `/blog/what-is-a-json-array` - Deep dive into array indexing, bracket structures, and mixed-type rules.
- `/blog/json-vs-xml` - Modern comparisons on mapping, speed, parsing complexity, and transmission overhead.

### Formatting & Compression
- `/blog/how-to-format-json` - Complete guide on pretty printing inside browsers, editors (VS Code), and CLI (`jq`).
- `/blog/json-pretty-print-explained` - Technical explanation of how computer parsers process whitespace serialization.
- `/blog/how-to-minify-json-for-production` - How lossless minification reduces network latency and bandwidth costs.

### Validation & Troubleshooting
- `/blog/how-to-validate-json` - Walkthrough on syntax checking using JS `try/catch` and online engines.
- `/blog/how-to-fix-invalid-json` - Practical solutions for trailing commas, single quotes, and missing brackets.
- `/blog/why-is-my-json-invalid` - Quick diagnostic checklists for runtime token errors.
- `/blog/how-to-escape-quotes-in-json` - How to write strings containing quotes, backslashes, and carriage returns.

### Integrations & Conversions
- `/blog/how-to-convert-json-to-csv` - How flat key-headers and data columns map to Excel and Google Sheets.
- `/blog/how-to-convert-csv-to-json` - Writing pure JavaScript state machines to split rows without breaking on cell commas.
- `/blog/how-to-read-a-json-file-online-safely` - Tips on inspecting database dumps without cloud storage leaks.
- `/blog/how-to-view-json-as-a-tree` - Techniques for rendering collapsed, searchable nested structures.
- `/blog/how-to-debug-api-json-responses` - Browser network tab tips, header tracking, and JavaScript logging methods.
- `/blog/json-for-rest-apis` - Best practices for API development: consistent camelCase keys and clean error wrapping.

---

## 📜 Metadata & Legal
- **About**: `/about` - Core mission statement emphasizing 100% browser isolation.
- **Contact**: `/contact` - Official channels for developers feedback and bug reports.
- **Source Repository**: `https://github.com/kejie1/json_utility_kit` - Public codebase for implementation review.
- **Privacy Policy**: `/privacy-policy` - Explicit guarantee of local computations and ad cookie transparency.
- **Terms of Service**: `/terms` - Provided as-is; data management guidelines.
